sentences.text: Solomon.
Cool.
Hey, good morning, Zach.
How are you?
Hey guys, good.
How are you?
Hello.
Good, hello, hello.
Yeah, doing good.
No, no complaints.
Close to Friday, long weekend, so… you know?
Where are you guys based?
We are both based out of Scottsdale.
What about you?
Nice.
I'm based in DC, and… Avni's in, San Francisco.
Yeah, nice.
Very cool.
You guys have any, plans for the long weekend?
I should.
I don't, but I should.
What about you, Albany?
I just moved to San Francisco, like, 2 weeks ago, so….
Bye.
I'm still trying to figure it out, but I think I… I'm seeing a friend, so… I didn't even realize it was Labor Day this weekend.
What about you guys?
No real plans for me.
Probably do a long hike or a long run on a Saturday with a friend of mine.
Cool.
I forgot it was Labor Day, too, actually.
I had some friends asking about, like, a pool party on Monday, and I completely forgot, but probably watching some college football for me, so excited for football season being back.
You've got a squad, you sound… you seem like a Texas guy, I'm gonna guess.
Hunter's a Texas guy, I'm Penn State, actually.
I'm originally from Pittsburgh, so not terribly far from DC, but….
Oh, nice.
Yeah.
Well, perfect.
Glad that we could get you both on the call.
Should be pretty casual, just a back-and-forth conversation today around, rent comp data, overall market analysis.
We'll walk you through our platform, the different reports, and some of the reporting we can provide for you all.

And then at the end, just come together collectively, and you guys give us your feedback, whether you think it's worth further pursuing, if you think it's a good fit, if you think we should get more eyes on it internally.
You just let us know.
How's that sound?
Good.
Did you guys, … who reached out to me?
I forget.
It was either, Hope or Sophia, I think.
Yeah, I forget.
One of them did.
They caught my attention, one, I've heard of you guys, so I want to hear about it, but … They asked me what I wanted data on, and I gave them a deal.
Is that… do you guys have that, or no?
Oh, yeah, we do.
Okay, cool.
… So yeah, I just want to see the platform, and I gave her… I think it was 1 East Delaware, right?
Yeah, sir, that's what I was gonna confirm.
That's the one that we have.
Cool.
Yeah, I just wanted to see… use that as, like, a… I don't know, example, or send me reports, or whatever, and … also, obviously, you know, hear about the transaction.
She looped me in, she asked me what deal I wanted to see, I told her when, and then she said, well, let's get on the call, so here we are.
Yeah, cool.
Yeah, we have that.
And then what I can do at the end of the call, too, is I can send you the, the raw reporting, like the exports that we have for that property as well.
But… So you, you said you heard about us, so how'd you hear about us?
Yeah, honestly, I forget.
I mean, I talk to people in the industry, but I've heard of you guys, and I think people have reached out over time.
We don't have a ton of people at our shop, so we don't… we don't talk to, like, a million different providers and things, and we might use you through our third-party management in certain aspects.
Who's your main competitor?
Like, who's your two, three, four main competitors?
I want to know if I, like, use them, or what the difference is.
I would say maybe, like, a year, two years ago, it was probably Radix.
Yeah, so I use them, I don't use them anymore.
I use Hello Data now.
Yeah, that would probably be the… Other company that comes up the most in deals, but … We could talk about the differences, but … I think our platform is similar to them, but it's a bit different.
So yeah, could definitely go over the differences there, if that's important to you.
Okay.
I'm curious, who are, some of the third-party, clients that you guys utilize?
Graystar Will Bridge, Bizuto… AMC, some locals, in-house management as well, so spread it out… spread… Spread it around the country.
Yeah, for sure.
Familiar with all of them.
And Justin and I are actually the, the sales reps over here that brought on the full, Willow Bridge portfolio.
Excellent.
Yeah, really big fan of those guys over there.
Yeah, the group.
Yeah, they're great people.
I'm curious, so, I'm assuming they're probably sending you, like, apartment IQ reports, is that correct?
Probably.
I should know the answer to that, but, probably.
Okay, but you guys don't actually have access to, like, the user interface and, like, dashboards?
No, I probably should, because I'm the one paying for it, it's not actually Willowbridge, but… yeah, I don't think so.
Okay.
That's not a problem.
If we get to it, we can always get you set up with, like, your own instance.
But, I'll go over, I guess outside of that, what other data platforms are you guys utilizing for just rent comp, or, like, overall market analysis?
You mentioned Teladata, but who else?
Yeah, we use… we really just use HelloData for underwriting exercises.
It's, like, relatively cheap.
for what we use it for, and I think it generally does an okay job, actually.
I like it more than Radix, and … they kind of scrape the internet, and you can, like, manipulate the data how you need to, … So yeah, I mean, I don't need…
the benefit is I don't need full, like, investor reporting.
I'm not going out to equity, trying to prove my thesis… I mean, I'm proving my thesis, and so is Avani, right?
Internally, but it's… we're not, like, raising equity and, like, creating a story and have it buttoned up.
We're truly just looking for…
well-summarized, believable market surveys that we can make investment decisions on, not go look at the prettiest one and report in verbiage and send that to our investors, so….
Yeah, that makes sense to me.
… Yeah, we could definitely get you covered there, so I'll give you kind of the overview and the different functionalities.

But, we've been around for the last 5 years, and we've been tracking all this publicly available data down to the unit level, over the last 5 years, so the good thing about that is you do get our entire like, historical database that you can query, 5 years of historicals to leverage for seasonality and trends, 3 functionalities in the tool, one of which is for the day-to-day operations.
You get a live data feed into your owned or managed assets, as well as each specific comp set you're tracking for each.
So this allows you to log in and see where each of your floor plans and units are positioned within that each specific comp set.
And that….
That's pulling from the website, or you already….
That is pulling directly from the online leasing section of the property websites.
Got it, and that's what Holo Data does, too, right?
They do ILS.
Oh, they do iOS?
ALSs, which has capped availability, that kind of skews occupancy, everything else kind of gets messed up from that.
Nice thing about going through the online leasing section is that and I'll just cover this, because this always comes up.
How do we get around hidden availability?
If you go through online leasing sections, or even, like, sitemaps, we have about, like, 600 different unique scrapers.
We've been doing this for a long time, so we have, like, found ways to get around that by typing in either exact unit numbers, because we'll show you.
We see every single unit come and go out of all these properties.
You can change move-in dates, amenity fixes, and those hidden units will actually pop up.
So we've done so many different data odyssey, tests against Helodata.
We never lost Willowbridge, Avenue 5, Cirrus Regis, they've all gone head-to-head.
We've never lost in accuracy.
So that's a big difference between us and them.
We go to online leasing, they go to ILSs, … Another functionality that we have is gonna be great for, like, acquisitions, new development.

You, query your entire database, takes about 3 seconds, you type in the property name or address, you'll get a real-time market survey, all the comps will be, added to that as well, as well as the historicals.
Nice thing about that is the data continues to update every single day as well.
Last functionality, very similar to CoStar or Market Analytics from RealPage.
It's gonna be all market, sub-market level information that you can drill down to the neighborhood.
You can even create different radiuses or boundaries.
We'll give you all new supply data, the different stages of construction pipeline, and then demographic information in that area to help you better understand rental profiles.
Okay.
So, we used to just be a market survey tool over, like, 2 years ago, then landing, like, some of the big partners, like Willowbridge and everyone else.
They pretty much said, hey, we use about 3, 4, 5 different data providers, and we don't love them.
Is there any way that you could help us kind of combine all this into one platform?
And then that's kind of what our team has done.
Yep.
So, quick overview.
I'm sure you'll have questions throughout, so just fire them off, and either Justin or myself can get them answered.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, let me walk you through it, and Zach, I'm gonna walk you through it from, like, my demo account, but we'll send you over that reporting for, for OneEast that I have set up here.
So I just have more features turned on in my demo account, so… I'm gonna walk you through it from… from there.
Okay.
I was gonna ask real quick, how many users do you guys have set up within, within Hello Data?
Is it, … Just you, or does the entire team have access?
How many people were in there?
Just one.
Okay, cool.
Yeah.
So yeah, like, I mean, we're gonna offer more features.
I mean, we're gonna have the market sub-market data, the construction, new supply, the demographics.
But I think the biggest thing is it's going to be more accurate.
So, just better accuracy, in regards to performance metrics.
You know, I know those guys don't show occupancy percentage, and the reason being is because they can't get an accurate availability number.

So, performance metrics will be better, leasing velocity's gonna be better, they overstate leasing velocity because they don't take into account applications and cancels and denials and things like that.
So, you know, as you're kind of reviewing our data with, with this one, with the deal that you sent over, take a look at theirs and, you know, obviously go head-to-head and apples to apples, but….
Okay.
Yeah, I'll give you the high level here.
So, part one, the My Properties section, this is just your market surveys.
So, each one of your properties would get their own individual property card, and all of the data is based off of that property-specific comp set.

So, if you click into here, again, just a reminder, the data is updated as of this morning, but what's nice is we do give you the ability to aggregate it over whatever timeframe is relevant to your conversation.
So, operationally, it probably makes sense to look day-to-day, but for an acquisition, maybe you want to look at over the last, you know, quarter, six months, a year, two years, etc.
So you can pick and choose whatever time frame you do care about.
But once you're in here, the very top's gonna be your main navigation, so each property has 4 reports associated with it.
The first one… Can you, just for this purpose, can you, filter to 12 months?
Yeah, so what this'll do is give you the aggregate of the 12, you know, of the 12 months.
I mean, I look at 3, 6, 12, but I never look at today's rents.
Makes sense.
It just… I think that's one of the biggest mistakes acquisition people make.
Sure.
Yeah, and this might even… I'm gonna jump ahead real quick, but this might even be a better view for you, this historical view.
So what this does is gives you a historical run, so you can take this… the time frame back as far as you want, you can select your interval.
What we're doing here is, in the first chart, is we're showing you his, historical rent, so both gross and net rents.
And right now, we're obviously looking at each individual's property's performance over that given time frame.
But if you'd like, you can toggle over and look at, you know, your target property's performance compared to the meaning of the comp set.
Yeah, so how… When you get into the data, and you have One beds, two beds, three beds, whatever, on every deal.
And some of them have… let's say in March, 26, you have… 1-1 bed.
10 two beds and 1 3 bed.
Yeah.
then… August of 26.
you have… 21 beds, no 2 beds, no 3 beds, right?
How does it aggregate or pull the data so you're not lopsided based on an expiration curve that is controlled by… management and the leases they sign.
Do you get what I'm saying?
Like.
Yeah, for sure.
Yep.
Yeah, so right now, it's gonna be a simple average.
It won't be a weighted average.
So what I would say, like, in this view right here, rather than looking at the data at the property level, to actually break it out by floor plan.
Yeah.
It's gotta be… I'm sure you guys know this, but… That's the biggest issue in all this data, which, like, makes me not want to pay up for any specific data source, because
you don't know what's real.
It always needs to be a weighted average month after month after month, otherwise it… It doesn't mean as much.
Right.

Yeah, I think one view that might be helpful for you, actually, and I'm gonna kind of jump ahead here, but if I go to this… let me show you this tool real quick, and it's kind of like a… An in-place rent rule type thing, so let me type in… and this is a different… this is a differentiator between us and HelloData, too, but if I look at Scottsdale, for example, this is where you get the high-level market data, all the other information, but what I'm showing you right now is every property in Scottsdale.
So, say you were looking at acquiring V-Tree, for example.
You click into here, and we'll give you all this information about V3.
So we can show you ownership group, you know, ownership or transactional history, we can show you management group, etc.
But if I come down below here, this… what might be helpful is this estimated rent rule.
So we can show you what's in place and everything broken out by floor plan.
you know, we can show you it at the property level as well, but this will be, you know, everything in place.
This will be, both rents, and price per foot.
So, I don't know if that's kind of what you're looking for?
Yeah, that's helpful.
And you can run that based on 3, 6, 12 months, or how does that work?
Or is that… like, how do you know that's in place?
Because you don't have the rent roll, right?
No, so the way it works is.
Over the last 5 years, we've essentially seen every unique unit in a property, so I can tell you unique units available, or if it's under application.
If it did lease, I can show you the date and the dollar amount that it leased for, you know, what concession was attached to it, how many days it's out on the market.
So we have all of that data just because we track this unit-level information every day, so we can back into a lot of these numbers, and that's also raw data we can feed to you as well.
And that's how you guys do it, though.
Like, you guys can figure it that way, looking back to unit by unit, whatever, four plans, and trying to triangulate a rent roll.
Yep.
Yeah.
Yeah, so, like, this is, like, one view in that.

I'm kind of… I know I'm jumping around here, but, like, even something else, too, like, in terms of how we aggregate the data, you know, like, this floor plan level report, like, I can show you… I'm gonna toggle on just a specific property for you.
But, like, this Camden Music Grow property, this property, you know, they've got, like, 18 or 17 different one-bedrooms in the property.
Yeah.
We can show you unit mix all the way down to this floor plan level, and then we can give you leased availability and rent trend data over whatever timeframe you care about as well.
That's good.
Yeah, so, I mean, we tracked… I mean, I can take all this data down to the actual unit level, and then we can obviously aggregate it all the way up to the property level.
Okay.
Like, if you want, like, the raw data, like, let me show you this too, like.
by clicking the Camden Music Row, and I click on All Units, so I can show you the unit mix, and then down below here, this is all 430 units in the property, and their current status.
So if they're available, great.
You know, if they are leased, though, like I was saying, we can show you when they were leased.
The dollar amount that we report for a transaction… for a transacted unit is based off of the last lowest price on the property website.
Yeah.
I assume you guys have, like… I'm… I'm sure it's not 100%, but I'm sure you guys have… try to cross-reference the data to a rent roll?
Like, what's the percent of accuracy, usually?
Yeah, it's, … I don't have a specific number for you, I could talk to our product team, because we have done it.
The issue is….
Kind of like that expiration curve like you were talking about.
And honestly, like, all the new supply hitting these markets, so… you know, if there is a 3-month lease, or a 6-month, or a 9-month lease signed, I mean, we just don't know about it.
Yeah.

I think what's good about it, though, is those should be… well, I guess I was gonna say they should be outliers, but if we would start throwing in those three 6-month leases, it's gonna skew the data big time.
Okay.
The consistency or the normalization of the data has been making sense, especially since, like, post-pandemic, we're back to some, you know, normalization in terms of trends and seasonality.
But I mean, like, I think Phoenix is a good example, I mean.
Well, Phoenix is in the shitter, isn't it?
Isn't rents going backwards, or no?

They are, but what I was gonna say is, I mean, it… they are… it is going backwards, and there's so much new supply hitting right now, that, I mean, even… all this supply is hitting, and it's not peak leasing season, so those expiration curves, I mean, you're getting some wild, you know, term length offers.
Yeah.
So that's… I don't even… I don't know if I can even call it an anomaly anymore, but that's… that's some stuff that we can't account for.
I mean, it's just, you know, that's….
Yeah, no, that's hard.
Yeah.
Stable market, though, is probably much easier, so….
Absolutely, yeah.
But, yeah, I mean, we have… we have the data every way… every which way possible.
Like I was saying, like, with HelloData, they don't show you occupancy percentage, because they can't get availability right.
So if you get… if you don't get availability right, you know, all these performance metrics are wrong.
So that's, like, you know, the quality of the data you're getting with us is much better.
But I usually have a financial… I usually have a T12 and a rent roll, so the rent roll shows me occupancy today, and the T12 shows me generally occupancy over that time period.
Yeah.
Are you getting that from those guys, or from, ….
I mean, the only reason I would use this… so there's two reasons to use this, right?
You're owning a deal, and you're operating it, and you want to look your comps and understand where they are, right?
Yep.
That's probably what Wilbridge and all of them do.
The real reason we use HelloData is not….
for any of that, it's for acquisitions, and when I do an acquisition, I always have the financials in a rent roll, so….
Do you get those from brokers, or from where?
Yeah, brokers, signed CAs, and every single deal we look at, we get the financials in a rent roll.
Yeah, makes sense.

Yeah, I mean, like, for us, for, like, the acquisition tool, what we have is research, and I'm assuming it's pretty similar to Hello Data, but what you do is you click on New Report, and then you type in the name or the address of whatever property you're looking to acquire, and you find it in the list, and then you click on it, and, like, 3 seconds, you have all that reporting that I just walked you through.
Yeah.
So, with us, we let you, you know, basically have access to our entire database just by typing in the name or the address of a property.
How much does it cost?
Yeah, it's a per seat, per seat per month model, so it's gonna be about the same as Hello Data, 300 bucks a month or so.
Because HelloData was pretty cheap, I thought.
Yeah, they… I'll tell you, I mean, yeah, they… they kind of undercut the market big time.

They were… they came out of nowhere and started charging, you know, 100 bucks a month per seed or whatever, but I think since they got acquired by Grace Hill that they're kind of taking them back to market, so….
Yeah, yeah, you might be right.
Yeah.
For what it's worth, too, just to show you this view, we do have, … Ownership history, or sorry, transaction history, and then we also have, like, cap rates in here as well.
So….
Yep.
We get all that information as well for you, but… Yeah, I think, ….
You get that out of, what, CoStar or RCA?
You got a partnership with RCA, yep.
Okay.
Yeah.
And then, for what it's worth, we also have forecasting coming.
So, we did, steal an economist from CoStar about 4 months ago.
Forecast is dropping here shortly after, Labor Day.
So, very excited about that.
And last part of this that might be beneficial for you, you know, I don't know exactly which markets you're looking to acquire in.
We have this market comparison dashboard.
So, like, for my example, I'm shooting the top 50 MSAs in the country, but you can pick and choose, you know, neighborhood zip codes, counties, etc.
You can select whatever time frame you want to view the data.
And then in here, we're giving you literally every bit of information possible.
So, how many units are in each market, you know, what rent growth is projected to be.
You know, lots of data in here, just to kind of keep a high-level pulse on maybe your next acquisition target.
What do you… like, when you filter this by rent growth, what pops up?
Naperville.
Yeah!
That's pretty damn close to where your, your property is, right?
That one, whatever you're underwriting?
Well, I… I don't know, this might be self-proclaimed, but I think we're the biggest landlord in Naperville.
Nice, that's awesome.
We have, like, 7 deals in Naperville, so… Nice.
But yeah, that other one is Chicago, so….
Yeah, cool.
How many….
I'm also a Bay Area firm.
Look at this, Avni, we're right at the top of the list.
Yeah, in Pittsburgh, right here, I'm a Pittsburgh guy.
Don't worry, we also own some shit at the bottom of the list, too, so….
How many units do you guys have in the portfolio total?
Where's… where's Salt Lake City on this?
They gotta be towards the bottom.
Let's see….
We have, we have 11,000-ish units, so….
Yeah.
They're not too bad.
Yeah, that looks right.
Yeah, about flat.
But…
Yeah, I mean, I think this… I think the… again, this report, what's nice is if you're familiar with CoStar, I mean, you know, this is every property in that area.
We have every single filtering option, so you can look by vintage, by total units, by property class, management company, ownership group, etc.
So, lots of ways to slice and dice this data.
Cool.
So….
You'll… if we like you, you'll have to buy our love.
So… No problem there.

Just cause… I mean, we're… we use all these things, but we're not, like, a… We're data-driven, but we're also, like, we're not, like… I don't know, we're not these huge institutions that, like, as I said, need to put the data together and go sell it to an investor, so, it's all important, but, … But yeah, at some point, we say, like, what we have is fine for X price, why are we gonna… Continue and pay a lot more, so….
Makes sense.
Well, yeah, we'll send you over that report.
I mean, like I said, compare what we got versus Hello Data.
The report that we have, I guess, let me ask you this, we're gonna… we were gonna send it as, like, a snapshot of today.
Do you want the 12-month look back, or I guess, what do you prefer there for the export that we send over?
Yeah, more so 12… definitely 12 months.
I mean, I usually look at 3, 6, 12.
I mean, today is good, too, but, like, usually I'm toggling those 3 or 4 metrics, so….
Awesome.
Cool.
Last thing for you, Zach, just curious if you're comfortable with sharing, what's the length left on the Hello Data contract?
I think it's month to month.
Okay.
I mean, I just, like, literally just went on there, like, I didn't even talk to a person, I just went on, signed to put my credit card in, and… Right.
I don't know, $200 or something?
Yeah.
Okay, good to know.
Yeah.
I'll, I'll get a follow-up email out shortly with the, report for you.
Cool.
Awesome.
Thank you both.
Thanks, guys.
See you later.
